name: Fiji
_editorial_notes: Researched 2026-07-21 from official .gov.fj sources plus closely-linked official sites (frcs.org.fj, feo.org.fj, hec.org.fj, fnu.ac.fj, tsls.com.fj, myfnpf.com.fj). Not rendered by the app -- kept here for future maintainers.

FNPF (Fiji National Provident Fund) contact details are unreliable enough that they've been deliberately left out entirely: a direct fetch of myfnpf.com.fj's own contact pages returned a bogus New York, USA address and US-format phone number -- almost certainly a rendering/template artifact, definitely not real. Secondary sources gave conflicting Suva addresses. Only the domain (myfnpf.com.fj) and general role are mentioned; a phone/address should be added only after a fresh direct confirmation.

Ministry of Lands and Mineral Resources' own site (lands.gov.fj) refused connection throughout research -- its entry below is reconstructed from search-indexed snippets, not a verbatim quote of a live page.
Land Transport Authority's specific pages (lta.com.fj/licensing, /registration) returned 404/empty content -- its contact number is sourced from a search snippet, not independently re-verified.

Hospital street addresses and direct phone numbers (CWM, Lautoka, Labasa) were not present on the Ministry of Health's own hospital pages when fetched directly -- those loaded as navigation menus only. The numbers here come from third-party directories, not the Ministry site itself.

Higher Education Commission's domain is hec.org.fj -- an older domain, fhec.org.fj, still appears in some search results under the same name but throws a TLS certificate error (it points at a mismatched server) and should be treated as defunct, not used.

The current Health Minister and other named officeholders change with Fiji's cabinet reshuffles faster than this file will be updated -- deliberately not included by name to avoid going stale; only agency names and contacts are listed.

No official, citizen-facing 'how to enroll your child in school' step-by-step guide was found on education.gov.fj -- only internal policy/data-system documentation. Not included here since it couldn't be confirmed.

items.name: Passport (new or renewal)
items.location: 40 Robertson Rd, Suva (regional offices in Nadi, Lautoka, Nausori, Labasa, Savusavu)
items.description: Ordinary processing: $206.50, 15 working days. Urgent: $283.85, 3 working days. As of late 2025, appointments are booked online rather than by walk-in.

items.name: Business registration
items.agency: Registrar of Companies, Ministry of Justice
items.location: Level 1, Suvavou House, Victoria Parade, Suva
items.description: Registers business names, companies, credit unions, and money lenders. Online registration and e-Profile available via roc.digital.gov.fj. Starting a business is two steps: business name registration here, then tax registration with FRCS.

items.name: Tax registration (TIN, VAT, returns)
items.location: Nasese, Suva (regional offices in Sigatoka, Nadi, Lautoka, Ba, Rakiraki, Nausori, Labasa, Savusavu, Levuka)
items.description: The Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) is compulsory for all Fiji citizens. Online services via the Taxpayer Online Services (TPOS) portal.

items.name: Birth, death, and marriage registration
items.agency: Registrar General's Office, Ministry of Justice
items.location: Level 1, Suvavou House, Victoria Parade, Suva
items.description: Births must be registered within 2 months, and registration is free within the first 12 months. Local citizens can also register via the Digital Fiji app. About 20 registration offices exist across Fiji.

items.name: Voter registration
items.location: 59-63 High Street, Toorak, Suva (also Lautoka and Labasa offices)
items.description: In person only, with an original birth certificate and current photo ID. Registering more than once is unlawful. Address changes must be reported within 3 months of moving.

items.name: Driver's license
items.description: Applicants must appear in person at an LTA branch for the licensing process.

items.name: Land transactions
items.agency: Ministry of Lands and Mineral Resources
items.location: iTaukei Trust Fund Building Complex, Nasova, Suva (divisional offices in Suva, Lautoka, and Labasa)
items.description: Includes an online myleaseinfo service for state-land lessees to check lease information.

items.name: Ministry of Health and Medical Services
items.description: Runs 3 divisional hospitals, 18 sub-divisional hospitals, and over 80 health centres across Fiji's Central, Eastern, Northern, and Western divisions plus Rotuma.

items.name: CWM (Colonial War Memorial) Hospital
items.description: Fiji's largest and oldest public hospital and primary tertiary referral centre, established 1923. A national teaching hospital partnered with Fiji National University.

items.name: Lautoka Hospital
items.description: Divisional hospital serving the Western Division.

items.name: Labasa Hospital
items.description: Divisional hospital serving the Northern Division.

items.name: Ministry of Education, Heritage and Arts
items.description: Runs national schooling (compulsory Year 1-12, tuition-free) and administers Fiji's Year 12 and Year 13 national exams directly -- there's no separate exams authority.

items.name: Higher Education Commission (HEC)
items.location: Level 1 Red Cross Building, 22 Gorrie Street, Suva
items.description: Regulates technical/vocational (TVET) qualifications under the Fiji National Qualifications Framework -- registers training providers and accredits courses. (Its domain is hec.org.fj -- an older fhec.org.fj domain is no longer valid.)

items.name: Fiji National University (FNU)
items.description: Fiji's main TVET and higher-education provider, offering roughly 80 TVET programmes from certificate to diploma level across five colleges.

items.name: Tertiary Scholarships and Loans Service (TSLS)
items.location: Suva (also offices in Lautoka and Labasa)
items.description: Fiji's government tertiary scholarship and student loan body. Applications via apply.tsls.com.fj.

sources.label: Ministry of Justice (Registrar of Companies, Registrar General)
